figures.caption: Figure 2: Detailed memory layout and bit-packing for environment entities. Metadata is compressed into 32-bit words to maximize L1 cache residency and minimize memory bandwidth overhead. Tile Flags: G – Global observed status, B – Blocking/Impassable, A – Aquatic, F – Flyable, and W – Walkable. AgentState Flags: bit 0 – Stuck, bit 1 – Walk, bit 2 – Fly, bit 3 – Swim. POIState Flags: M – Moves (dynamic entity), S – Saved/Rescued, and F – Found/Discovered. The 20-bit masks allow for O(1) visibility and capability checks via bitwise AND/OR operations across up to 20 agents.
Figure 6: Phase 1 throughput comparison for C++ execution utilizing different core-affinity and initialization strategies on AMD EPYC. Strict thread pinning severely reduced total SPS throughput.
Table 5: Hyperparameters used for PPO, DQN, and SAC in the multi-agent test environments.

Figure 3: Diagram standard RL pipeline: Env Takes Step(s) (blue), then transfers observation to GPU (blue arrow) to take action(s) (orange) before those actions are transported back to the CPU (orange arrow). By using parallel environments we amortize each step’s overhead cost and by using pinned memory we minimize transfer latency.
Figure 7: Episodic return trajectories demonstrating the sample complexity demands of various architectures operating over 1 million steps in highly parallelized zero-copy environments.
Figure 1: Overview of Emergency Response and Monitoring Scenarios (Single Row)
Figure 10: Throughput scaling profiles for the Intel 4-Core Laptop.
Figure 4: Laptop Speedtest on native single threaded numpy environment
Figure 9: Throughput scaling profiles for the AMD EPYC 7282.

Table 3: Hardware optimization result matrix comparing CPU contention variables against SPS throughput and GPU utilization. Values represent Mean ± SEM for n = 6 trials. The 32-thread subset demonstrates higher per-core efficiency by avoiding Cross-Fabric interconnect latency.
Figure 5: Performance scaling evaluation of progressive architectural improvements against varying environment counts during the ablation study.
Table 1: Physical Memory Layout of a Single Environment Stride
Figure 8: Throughput scaling profiles for the AMD Ryzen 9950x.
